q: A 4 kg block is pulled along a horizontal surface by a 20 N force acting at 37° above horizontal. If coefficient of friction is 0.25, acceleration of the block is approximately:
exp: Horizontal force = 20cos37° = 16 N. Normal = 40 − 12 = 28 N, friction = 7 N. Net force = 9 N, so a = 9/4 ≈ 2.25 m/s^2 ≈ nearest 2.8.

q: A particle moves under a force F = 6x N, where x is displacement in meter. Work done in moving particle from x = 0 to x = 2 m is:
exp: W = ∫Fdx = ∫6x dx from 0 to 2 = 12 J.

q: A body projected vertically upward with kinetic energy 400 J reaches maximum height h. Neglecting air resistance, potential energy at h is:
exp: Mechanical energy remains conserved, so PE at top = initial KE.

q: A spring of force constant 200 N/m is compressed by 10 cm. Energy stored is:
A 2 kg body moving with velocity 6 m/s collides head-on elastically with a stationary 3 kg body. Velocity of first body after collision is:
exp: Using elastic collision formula: v1 = [(m1−m2)/(m1+m2)]u1 = (-1/5)×6 = -1.2 m/s.

q: A machine performs 2.4 × 10^5 J work in 4 minutes. Its power output is:
The work done by centripetal force during uniform circular motion is:
exp: Centripetal force is perpendicular to displacement, so work done is zero.

q: A body of mass m falls freely from height h. Its kinetic energy after falling distance h/2 is:
exp: Loss in potential energy equals gain in kinetic energy.

q: Which graph represents kinetic energy versus momentum correctly?
exp: KE = p^2/2m, so KE is proportional to square of momentum.

q: Assertion (A): Work done by friction can be positive. Reason (R): Friction always opposes motion.
opts: Both A and R true, R explains A
Both A and R true, but R does not explain A
exp: Friction can aid motion in some cases, so work by friction may be positive.

q: A 5 kg object slides down a frictionless incline of vertical height 20 m. Speed at bottom is:
A force of 8 N produces displacement x = 3t^2 m in its direction. Power at t = 2 s is:
exp: v = dx/dt = 6t = 12 m/s. Power = Fv = 8×12 = 96 W.

q: A conservative force does 40 J work in moving a particle from A to B. Work done in returning from B to A is:
exp: Work by conservative force reverses sign on reversing path.

q: A body of mass 2 kg moving at 5 m/s is stopped by a retarding force in 2 s. Average power delivered by retarding force is:
exp: Loss in KE = 25 J over 2 s, so power = -12.5 W.

q: A man carries a suitcase on his head while walking on a horizontal road. Work done by man on suitcase is:
exp: Force is vertical while displacement is horizontal.

q: A block attached to a spring executes SHM. Total mechanical energy depends on:
exp: E = (1/2)kA^2, depends on amplitude.

q: A body moving with speed v has kinetic energy K. If its momentum doubles, kinetic energy becomes:
exp: K = p^2/2m. Doubling momentum quadruples kinetic energy.

q: A force-displacement graph is triangular with base 4 m and height 10 N. Work done is:
exp: Work equals area under graph = (1/2)×4×10 = 20 J.

q: The escape speed from Earth is ve. A body projected with speed ve/2 has total mechanical energy:
exp: Only at escape speed total energy becomes zero.

q: A body is moving on a rough horizontal surface. Which quantity remains constant if friction acts?
exp: Mass remains unchanged while energy and momentum change.

q: A 1000 kg car moving at 20 m/s is stopped in 5 s. Average braking power is:
exp: Initial KE = 2×10^5 J. Power = 2×10^5 / 5 = 40 kW.

q: A particle moves in x-direction under force F = 4 − x^2. At which x is force zero?
Which of the following quantities can never be negative?
exp: Kinetic energy depends on square of speed and is always non-negative.

q: A body slides down from rest along a smooth inclined plane making angle 30° with horizontal. Ratio of kinetic energy to potential energy lost after sliding distance s is:
exp: On smooth plane, loss in PE equals gain in KE.

q: Two bodies of masses m and 4m have same kinetic energy. Ratio of their momenta is:
exp: p = √(2mK), so momentum ∝ √m.

q: A force acting on a particle varies with displacement as F = ax + b. Work done from x = 0 to x = d is:
exp: W = ∫(ax+b)dx = (1/2)ad^2 + bd.

q: The efficiency of a machine is 80%. If input power is 2 kW, output power is:
exp: Efficiency = output/input = 0.8 ⇒ output = 1.6 kW.

q: A bullet fired into a wooden block embedded in wall comes to rest after penetrating 10 cm. If initial kinetic energy is 500 J, average retarding force is:
exp: Work done by resistive force = KE. F×0.1 = 500 ⇒ F = 5000 N.

q: A particle moves in a straight line under conservative force. Total mechanical energy:
exp: Conservative forces conserve mechanical energy.

q: A body projected upward reaches maximum height in 4 s. Ratio of kinetic energy after 2 s to initial kinetic energy is:
exp: At half the ascent time, velocity becomes half. KE ∝ v^2, so ratio = 1/4.

q: Power delivered by a force F to a particle moving with velocity v at angle θ is:
exp: Power = F·v = Fv cosθ.

q: A lift of mass 500 kg is raised upward with acceleration 2 m/s^2. Power delivered by motor at speed 3 m/s is:
exp: Force = m(g+a)=500×12=6000 N. Power = Fv = 18000 W.

q: A particle moves in a circle at constant speed. Which statement is correct?
opts: Power delivered by centripetal force is zero
exp: Force is perpendicular to velocity, so instantaneous power is zero.

q: A body of mass m has momentum p. Its kinetic energy is:
exp: Standard relation between kinetic energy and momentum.

q: Assertion (A): Potential energy can be negative. Reason (R): Zero level of potential energy is arbitrary.
opts: Both A and R true and R explains A
Both A and R true but R does not explain A
exp: Potential energy depends on chosen reference level.

q: A body moving on rough surface stops after traveling distance d. If initial speed doubles, stopping distance becomes:
exp: From v^2 = u^2 − 2as, stopping distance ∝ u^2.

q: A constant force acts on a body. Which graph is linear?
exp: W = Fs when force is constant.

q: A body falls from height h. Its kinetic energy at height h/3 from ground is:
exp: It has fallen distance 2h/3, so KE gained = 2mgh/3.

q: The slope of work-energy graph gives:
exp: Since dW/dx = F, slope gives force.

q: A force of 50 N stretches a spring by 0.2 m. Energy stored in spring is:
exp: Average force = 25 N, so energy = 25×0.2 = 5 J.

q: A body of mass 1 kg moving with velocity 10 m/s explodes into two equal parts moving perpendicular to each other with equal speeds. Speed of each fragment is:
exp: Using conservation of momentum vectorially: √2(m/2)v = 10 ⇒ v = 14.1 m/s.

q: A particle is acted upon by a variable force. Work done equals:
exp: Work is given by integral of force over displacement.

q: A body is projected upward with speed 30 m/s. Ignoring air resistance, speed after 2 s is:
exp: v = u − gt = 30 − 20 = 10 m/s approximately.

q: In an elastic collision between two bodies:
opts: Both momentum and kinetic energy conserved
exp: Elastic collision conserves both momentum and kinetic energy.

q: The work done by gravitational force around a closed path is:
exp: Gravitational force is conservative.

q: A body of mass 2 kg moving with speed 5 m/s experiences constant opposing force 10 N. Distance traveled before stopping is:
exp: Initial KE = 25 J. Work by friction = Fs = 25 ⇒ s = 2.5 m.

q: A cyclist moving on horizontal road doubles his speed. His kinetic energy becomes:
exp: KE ∝ v^2.

q: A body slides down an incline with friction. Which quantity decreases?
exp: Part of mechanical energy converts into heat.

q: The maximum compression produced in a spring is x when body of mass m moving with speed v strikes it. If speed doubles, compression becomes:
A 60 kg man climbs stairs of height 20 m in 25 s. Power developed is approximately:
